name: PEARL rapamycin healthspan trial
summary: Completed 48-week decentralized double-blind randomized placebo-controlled trial of weekly low-dose rapamycin in healthy normative-aging adults, assessing visceral fat, body composition, blood biomarkers, surveys, and safety.
population: Healthy normative-aging adults in the PEARL trial; ClinicalTrials.gov lists 129 enrolled and the peer-reviewed analysis reports weekly 5 mg or 10 mg rapamycin versus placebo.

trial_details.horizon_note: Result-bearing human healthspan and safety context; not a longevity, mortality, or definitive disease-prevention trial.
trial_details.why_it_matters: PEARL is one of the larger and longer published low-dose rapamycin studies in healthy adults and directly addresses safety plus healthspan metrics, while its null primary visceral-fat endpoint and selective secondary/subgroup signals keep interpretation cautious.
